answer:
**Allama Iqbal's *Bang-e-Dara* (divided into three parts in this corpus) is a collection of Urdu poems, with each chapter representing an individual poem.**[^1][^2][^3] Several poems explicitly take a specific person (historical, contemporary, or legendary) as their addressee or central subject, as indicated by their titles and content. Here are the relevant ones:

- **"Abdul Qadir Ke Naam"** (*Bang-e-Dara Part 2*, Chapter 8): Addressed to (or "in the name of") Abdul Qadir, urging him to arise and illuminate the assembly with fiery eloquence.[^4]
- **"Swami Ram Tirath"** (*Bang-e-Dara Part 2*, Chapter 23): Subject is Swami Ram Tirath, the Indian poet and philosopher, praised for his spiritual dissolution ("annihilated" in the river, becoming a priceless pearl).[^5]
- **"Sayed Ki Loh-e-Turbat"** (*Bang-e-Dara Part 1*, Chapter 38): Centers on a Sayed (a descendant of the Prophet), imagined speaking from his tombstone/epitaph, offering advice on guidance, unity, and courage.[^6]
- **"Ram"** (*Bang-e-Dara Part 3*, Chapter 17): Subject is Ram (Rama), celebrated as a spiritual guide and symbol of Hindustan's wisdom and valor.[^7]
- **"Tazmeen bar sher-e Aneesi Shamlu"** (*Bang-e-Dara Part 3*, Chapter 24): A *tazmeen* (poetic extension) on a couplet by Aneesi Shamlu, incorporating voices critiquing a wayward lover (Qais/Majnun).[^8]

No other poems in the corpus directly name a specific individual in their title or as the clear addressee/subject based on chapter listings and sampled content. Broader addresses (e.g., to youth in "Khitab ba Jawanan-e-Islam" or divine figures) do not qualify as "specific person." Searches for direct vocatives like "O [name]" or "to the [person]" in English translations yielded no additional matches.[^9]

[^1]: *Bang-e-Dara Part 1*, [Chapter 1](https://falsafa-corpus.com/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-1-55b25c/01-ajab-waaiz-ki-deen-daari-hai-ya-rabb/translation/).
[^2]: *Bang-e-Dara Part 2*, [Chapter 1](https://falsafa-corpus.com/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-2-9bc58c/01-chamak-teri-ayaan-bijli-mein-aatish-mein-sharare-m/translation/).
[^3]: *Bang-e-Dara Part 3*, [Chapter 1](https://falsafa-corpus.com/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-3-cc61e8/01-bazm-e-anjum/translation/).
[^4]: *Bang-e-Dara Part 2*, Chapter 8, [full chapter](https://falsafa-corpus.com/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-2-9bc58c/08-abdul-qadir-ke-naam/translation/).
[^5]: *Bang-e-Dara Part 2*, Chapter 23, [full chapter](https://falsafa-corpus.com/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-2-9bc58c/23-swami-ram-tirath/translation/).
[^6]: *Bang-e-Dara Part 1*, Chapter 38, [full chapter](https://falsafa-corpus.com/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-1-55b25c/38-sayed-ki-loh-e-turbat/translation/).
[^7]: *Bang-e-Dara Part 3*, Chapter 17, [full chapter](https://falsafa-corpus.com/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-3-cc61e8/17-ram/translation/).
[^8]: *Bang-e-Dara Part 3*, Chapter 24, [full chapter](https://falsafa-corpus.com/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-3-cc61e8/24-tazmeen-bar-sher-e-aneesi-shamlu/translation/).
[^9]: Corpus search results for English translations (e.g., "Abdul Qadir", "O ", "to the").
